name: Fried Turkey
recipeInstructions:
1. Mix all ingredients (except broth) in a large saucepan over low heat.
2. Add the chicken broth to the pan, which will approximately double the amount of mixture.
3. Let simmer for 5 minutes, then cool to room temperature.
4. Wash the turkey thoroughly.
5. Using a large syringe, inject the cooled mixture into all meaty areas of the turkey (breast, wings, drumsticks, thighs, etc.).
6. Let marinate in refrigerator overnight.
7. Heat 5 gallons of cooking oil (preferably peanut oil) to 300 degrees F in a large pot (35 to 45 quarts).
8. Lower the whole turkey into hot oil slowly and carefully.
9. Cover the pot and check the oil temperature every few minutes, maintaining a range of 280-310 degrees.
10. Cook for exactly 3.5 minutes per pound of turkey weight (e.g., a 10 lb turkey will cook in 35 minutes).
11. As soon as the cooking time is up, remove the turkey, wrap it in aluminum foil.
12. After at least 15 minutes, slice the turkey as you would a baked one.
